Listen, we come all the time and we appreciate the “vouchers” after the reoccurring mistakes but I would rather have my order correct to enjoy it at home as a meal-which is why we order form you. I ordered a pasta with chicken cutlets added last night and unfortunately the chicken was rubbery again. It’s a bummer because I had the chicken one time and it was delicious but the last couple times it’s been rubbery.  I wanted to let you know so you can share with the kitchen.   Thank you!

name: Jennifer
text: Hi! I just wanted to give a little feedback on some issues with our order today.   We had the family meal last week and wanted to try it again, so ordered it with a different pasta tonight.   Both times, the container of pasta was on its side or upside down in the bag. Since the lid has a vented opening, that also meant the bag ended up soaked in sauce. The first time, I thought it must have been my fault for not holding the bag correctly. The second time, I saw the the pasta was already on its side and leaking when they handed me the bag. The dimensions of the bag, and the fact that they place the box of pasta on top of the plastic cover of the salad container with nothing to bolster it guarantees that the pasta container will slide and tip on its side -- even when it's first handed to you. Please, please do something about this.   Also, we paid for extra salad dressing and they forgot to include it.   Today the pasta was gummy and gluey. (Last week it was very good.)  The staff was VERY friendly, helpful and kind but they were super busy so I could see how some details could be missed.   The pizza was fantastic. The ceasar salad was excellent (we just wanted our extra dressing since we paid for it.)

I called as soon I I got home (I live 12 minutes away) and the manager said it was unacceptable and offered to fully refund my meal. It’s frustrating I wasn’t offered that plus the option to pick up the forgotten chicken cutlet but happy he at least refunded me

name: Azi Kiani
text: salty salad dressings. old lettuce

name: Sam Megna
text: The sandwich was okay compared to the first time I got it. The bread seemed to be stale and there was an unusual amount of mayo on the sandwich

name: Bobby Masak
text: Orange chicken doesn’t seem to have the orange glaze on it.

name: Tuesday West
text: Meant to put this in from my last order. Went in and bought a cookie from the Flour Shop. It’s okay, but lately the cookies have been lacking flavor & chewiness. Chewiness may not be what it’s going for, but the last 2 cookies have just tasted like flour without any real flavor. Also, the rice lately has been very very salty. Not upset or anything, just thought I’d pass along some thoughts. Everything else (including service) was phenomenal as usual :)

name: China Short
text: Hi, sandwiches were much better this time compared to the last two times, however I ordered two chocolate chip cookies and got the corn ones instead.

name: gabriella de
text: there was a staple inside my wrap

name: Susan Kovinsky
text: everything was good but i was shorted 1 cookie

name: Anna Perez
text: Our mix tape order was off today. There were too many fries  with our chicken tender order. Also, the French fries were hard and stale today.   Mini Kabob was good. Mini Kabob is often consistent and good.

name: David Pardo
text: Food was better than average. Radio was too loud and bad selection of music. Some of the dry products on the table could be better

name: Michael Daniels
text: I ordered chocolate chip cookies and received something else.

name: Marcelle Heimdal
text: The wrap was good but the burger was terrible. The avocado on the burger was hard and much of it was black with rot. The bun wasn’t good. The patty was greasy and very salty.

name: Karen Magon
text: I thought I had signed up for the 10%  discount but it didn’t show up when I placed my order . Also a 10% tip was incorrectly added to my order. However, Sarah at the restaurant saved the day by nicely and quickly taking care of it by adding the discount and refunding the tip. I appreciate your having an employee who understands the importance of customer service and who was able to problem solve. Kudos to Sarah!

name: Gianna Spiro
text: Super sad you forgot my fries.  Veggie burger was saltier than usual.

name: Monica Murrietta
text: Every time I get a discount code or sign up for texting, nothing works. People in the store aren’t sure how to handle. Other than that, the food is great!

name: Sam Megna
text: I thoroughly enjoyed the chicken sandwich and the fires were much better today. The bun however on the chicken sandwich was very dry almost stale it really took away from the good chicken which is unfortunate.

name: Mikaella Villepigue
text: I’ve eaten here a couple times and it’s been excellent. I tried a new dish today, Mama Alva’s Favorite Plate with chicken. The chicken was mostly fat and unable to eat. All of the flavors were delicious, but I was disappointed and unable to enjoy the full meal.

name: Shinal Patel
text: My child’s Mac n cheese I ordered was not in the bag. The waffle fries were mostly small bits and were stale. We’ve eaten here 3 times in the past month and this wasn’t the best experience.

name: Alice Chary
text: The website is difficult to use

name: Danielle Shap
text: Received the wrong dressing

name: Hannah Wozniak
text: Missing the rice but the workers are so nice

name: Daniel Rosen
text: Add ''on the side'' for ingredients in the online menu

name: Emily Feuer
text: The fries we ordered with our chicken meal were hard as a rock. Unfortunately we could barely bite into them.

name: Alison Nutter
text: Chicken was undercooked. Salmon was a very small portion.

name: Timothy Maki
text: When placing my online order, there was no option/tab showing for Flour Shop so I could not add it to my cart. In the store, the cookies were available, but because the cookies are something I look forward to, I almost didn’t complete my order when I realized I couldn’t order one.

name: Nicole Elias
text: The tuna salad sandwich is different but not in a good way. Very disappointing 😞

name: Kitti McMeel
text: Food was not good, it was a disappointment:(  Salmon was rubbery, croutons were soggy.. ugh.   I’ve ordered many things, this was not good and would never order again.

name: Ravinder Sharan
text: My vegetarian burger was overdone. Other items were fine.

name: Nicole Schussel
text: Always the friendliest staff! I felt like the hotel club sandwich was different than when I previously had it?

name: Rani Bijlani
text: I ordered a mini kebab sandwich with the kale falafel and no tzaki mast. But it was in there. I have dietary restrictions and sadly couldn’t eat it.

name: Erin Silverman
text: Online ordering system want working right

name: Sam Megna
text: Loved the club sandwich. It was extremely flavorful and the bread held up nice and didn’t get soggy. I was a little sad when I didn’t like the chicken strips but the sandwich was great

name: Susan Craig
text: Chicken in the Kung Pao was ‘tough’ and hard to chew. The dish was a bit sloppy and way too much onion. Orange chicken was OK but too much better. I’ve had better when ordering same dishes at your restaurant. Seems like food comes out different based on who is in the kitchen.

name: Rebecca Jacobs
text: Food was good, but we requested grilled chicken on our sandwich and it came crispy

name: Genie Agins
text: I must be crazy to keep ordering Gabby’s green burrito bowl, but when it’s good, it’s amazing. However, it can be hit or miss. The last time I ordered it, it was perfect. This time, chicken tasted strange, with small bone shards and the slaw was full of sand-not edible. I am gluten free, so haven’t tried the sandwiches or salads actually. This my last time ordering from Neighborly, especially when I’m hungry and expecting something good. I’m a long time customer, thought if I gave it more time, the food would become more consistent, obviously not. Sorry, the concept for this bowl is good, too bad.

name: Lisa Widejkis
text: The dumplings need to be a little better flavor

name: Lee Ginsburg
text: Received the incorrect sandwich

name: Alyssa Morris
text: Forgot sauces

name: Becky Salan-Motta
text: Paid for extra dressing for the La chop salad and Chinese chicken.  Received two extra for Chinese and no extra for La Chop.  More care in filling orders. Salads are delicious though.

name: John
text: Food was good but fries were basically inedible. Super overcooked and too crispy.

name: China Short
text: I love Neighborly, but something has definitely changed with the pizza crust— less thick and fluffy, and overall pizza presentation was Agee’s rather than the incredible first pizza I got with you.

name: Misty Lyons
text: Fries were undercooked and floppy. A bit greasy too. We’ve had them before and they were good!

name: Sam Megna
text: The fries were so dry and not good. The chicken tenders were way too salty as well. Want to try again but I’m worried and don’t want to waste the money

name: Kyle Lagana
text: Pizza was pretty good but a little too crispy. Overall solid experience but not up to par with other 20$ pizzas

name: Walker Orthodontics
text: We ordered a chocolate chip cookie and got a different kind of cookie, I think it's a cornflake cookie, was looking forward to the chocolate chip :(

name: Kelly Ungaro
text: The wrap changed since you opened. No longer has bacon and good tortilla. Very soggy and plain for that price

name: Jenny Hyams
text: Love the girl who works on the orders, she's really on it. Still have to say there's never enough shaved parmesan on the Cheese Store Caesar salad!

name: debbie newell
text: not being able to pay cash was definitely a disadvantage. being given room temp water wasn't good. for the price the turkey sandwich i got to go was horrible for $16. personally i wont be back

name: Lindsay Nielsen
text: Please please please offer a way for someone to bring your carry out food to the car. It gets so crowded in there and it’s a huge hassle to unload kids from the car to pick up food.

name: Lisa Miranda
text: You could allow phone orders; offer rewards; have an app

name: Karen Bradley
text: Disappointed. Neither thing I ordered was particularly special. Chicken sandwich was pretty basic and not very tasty. It actually had an odd stale oil taste. I don’t know why I thought the cookies would be warm but I did and they were not. Everyone was friendly and my order was prompt which I appreciate.

name: Tyler Norlund
text: Nothing

name: Margo Moses
text: We order from you regularly and I decided to try the chicken pesto for the first time but it wasn’t good. Barely any pesto on it and what little there was all soaked into the bread. 2 pieces of tomato. Would not order again. Also asked for champagne vinaigrette and it said right on order form but we got send red wine instead.

name: Olga Malamud
text: Wrong dressing provided on market salad. Discovered after mixing. Not a dressing we like.

name: Lilly Alexander
text: I tried to Call to speak to a manager no one picked up.

name: Mike Kennedy
text: Didn't send me all of what I ordered. I didn't know how to contact anyone to get what was missed or be refunded.

name: Arielle Feldman
text: I ordered a cheese quesadilla but was given a chicken quesadilla. Unfortunately  didn’t know until I got home and my daughter wouldn’t eat it.

name: Michael Lewis
text: Just a little more orange chicken in the family meal, please!

name: Brett Lantz
text: You forgot to put the Caesar dressing in the Togo box and the food was 15 minutes late

name: Karen Deeds
text: Broccoli dish not good.  I thought it was supposed to be roasted.  It was undercooked and had an unpleasant taste.

name: Heather MacMillan
text: Ordered mushrooms got chicken. Couldn't eat.

name: Colleen Walczykowski
text: The wrap is always sooooo good. Today it wasn’t as much. Seems like it was put together in a hurry.

name: Michelle Fitzgerald
text: Food was great. Luckily it was not hot food. Order took 20 min longer to arrive than initially quoted. Received at 8:16 quoted 7:54

name: Jennifer Terrill
text: I tried to check out at least twice and it said I could not switch basket owners? So I abandoned the order and went to Mendocino Farms for a salad.

name: Shaina Houston
text: Order on wrap wrong, asked for no kale and roast chicken… received kale, crispy chicken and no croutons.  Appreciate honoring the customizations selected.

name: Alejandro Paredes
text: Allow for ordering of Saturdays online (pickup only) and allow for ''no butter'' option on sandwiches.

name: LESLIE ALCORN
text: I ordered my food with no mushrooms. I have an allergy and they were added in anyways. All the other modifications that were made were followed. I had to throw it all away. I would like a refund

name: Edward Rouse
text: There was one item missing from my order.  I called and went back and they made it right. They included coupons for future use and some cookies!  It was a good recovery for the missed items. Thank you.

name: Cindy Pressman
text: Unfortunately our order from Mixtape was not great. Our first experience was amazing but this order just wasn’t very good. The quality was off.

name: Melanie Csellak
text: We did not receive the chicken strips meal we ordered. I called the restaurant but have not received a call back.

name: Chris Parsons
text: The orange chicken was very dry. Zero sauce present.   The rice sides were cold & hard like the rice was made hours before and sat in a steam kettle for hours.   The cookies are flavorless, not remotely sweet. Its harder to make cookies flavorless than it is to make them properly. There is no sign of sugar taste to any of them.   The kids tenders came with cold fries & dry chicken which my child politely refused. (He is 3) He literally has eaten our dogs treats left behind but would not touch the chicken

name: Sara Hershman
text: It is unfortunate that there is no indication that the lemon pasta was spicy. It was so spicy, it was basically inedible. The red pepper flakes were not noted in the ingredients and there was no option online to remove it. We ordered because we had a voucher from the last experience which was not good. Was hoping for better.

name: Patrick Kane
text: Food was great at usual!  Sandwiches appear to be getting a little smaller though.   Please keep providing the great quality food and good portions that you have been.  Thanks!

name: Trinh McDermott
text: I ordered the better tender box set with a Mountain Valley sparking water. The chicken were 3 tiny pieces so I thought I had made a mistake that I ordered a kid meal. It wasn't even a kid meal. I went all the way home to realize my drink wasn't in the bag. I went all the way back and a worker told me it should they're too busy today so someone had forgot. So much time wasted going back and forth.

name: Scott Foster
text: You have a great variety. We had the family dinner and chose the lemon ricotta pasta with a chicken cutlet, caesar salad and ll capo pizza. Loved the salad, enjoyed the pizza, but the chicken cutlet was dry, and the pasta wasn't a hit-perhaps just our taste. Ordering went well, and the pickup and friendliness of the staff was great.

name: Nico McGuire
text: Bag had completely soaked thru due to poorly insulated containers. Got all over clothes and car interior. This was noticed right as I got to my car, meaning it had already been soaked thru even while waiting in the restaurant. Yet it wasn’t noticed.

name: David Vielmetter
text: Pretty crowded. I guess it’s better than social monks crowd.

name: Jenny Hyams
text: It was a little crazy right out of the gate this morning. Was given wrong order, but I caught it before I got back in my car. Would like to see more shaved parmesan on the Cheese Store Caesar salad (which I love and buy every week)

name: Alycia Marrapodi
text: My LA Chop was missing the salami tonight for some reason. Otherwise loved it.

name: Kyle Lagana
text: Fries were way too well done. Felt like a big potato chip

name: Melanie DAgati
text: Time management would be good. Inside waiting for a take out order shouldn’t be longer than 10 minutes and this is the second time that happened and I felt like there were a lot of people working and some seemed confused so time management could improve. Some some extra training.

name: Jessica Diamond
text: The kids pasta was overcooked and inedible - we had to throw it away. The pesto had so much cheese on it that it also was inedible.

name: Stuart Rosenblum
text: N/A.

text: The pizzas were delicious but a little pricey

name: Melisa Giuffrida
text: Please get the order Correct. We modified sandwich with no bacon and it came with bacon.

name: James R
text: i had trouble navigating the website on mobile and i had to hit ''show desktop site'' in safari on iOS to get it to work properly.

name: Rachel Yohanan
text: Mini kabob order wasn’t great. Kabob wrap had barely any meat and was loaded with peppers. The cheeseburger wrap had no cheese so it was very dry and inedible.   Cheese store pizza was awesome.

name: Misty Lyons
text: Food wasn’t warm. Not sure if that’s neighborly or door dash

name: Sara Hershman
text: Signed up online with email and mobile and couldn’t get the discount. Also sandwich was prepared incorrectly

name: Tyler Wade
text: Our kids pizza didn’t show up and we were missing 3 of the dressings and sauces

name: Kim
text: The fries were pathetic really. After a few on the top they were small very small pieces. Threw them out.

name: Trinh McDermott
text: Drunken noodle is usually a spicy noodle dish but this one is a bit too sweet for me. It would be better if you ask the customer how spicy they want it to me. Plus, my chicken is not thin so it is a bit chewy.

name: Jason DiVenere
text: I wanted to say no mushrooms but there was no ability to do that when ordering through website so I had to call and talk to someone

name: Patricia Lillard
text: Bread was supposed to be lightly toasted- it wasn’t toasted T all!

name: Ryan Dewey
text: I’d say with this order it nailed it. Overall consistency overall on the food and portions thereof. It has been a bit of an area to improve on over the numerous amount of times we’ve been there.

name: Azi Kiani
text: mix take was stale and salty

name: Jennifer Terrill
text: Somehow and extra 2.00 was added to my tip on the app

name: Eileen Kakoyiannis
text: chicken cutlet always runs out by dinner

name: Shelley Riggs
text: More personalized service. The ability to order and pay cash upon pick up.

name: TEST DO NOT MAKE
text: Our kids loved the Only The Classics Crispy Chicken Sandwich!

name: Maggi Mahoney
text: Chicken burger was completely raw. My son took 2 bites and we are very concerned about salmonella. I have pictures.

name: Andrea
text: I ordered the mini kabob which did not have the sauces included that are usually put into the wrap. The slaw also had a more raw taste. It seems as though the individual whom prepared the wrap missed a few ingredients.

name: Deanna Marek
text: There wasn’t much chicken and needs more vegetables and less rice

name: Tina Bloss
text: My food was wrong… I called but no answer

name: shepard wallace
text: Address ux at beginning awkward

name: Debbie Bension
text: Service has gone way down- questions not answered

name: Meena Ramakrishnan
text: My kabob wrap had the wrong protein.  It was supposed to be crispy chicken, but I received the fire roasted chicken thigh.

name: Jacqueline Ramirez
text: Ordered the pesto chicken sandwich      for pickup and the bread was soggy by the time I got home. It was too greasy for my liking. A little disappointing for a $16 sandwich.   I would also appreciate it napkins were included in the to go bags.

name: Claire Farne
text: The chicken was hard and it seems liked it wasn’t cooked. I usually order the spicy chili chicken wonton which is my favorite. Today it tasted different. I was disappointed with my lunch today.

name: Keeya Luney
text: My chicken was rubbery so I can’t even enjoy my meal.

name: Christine Callahan
text: The experience is a little all over the place. I picked up a few pantry items and one staff member took them to ring me up though I wasn’t quite ready. He put the stuff down in a random place in the store. Then began helping someone else. Then I wanted to check out so it was someone else helping me and they didn’t know where he put my stuff. It’s like different people in different locations of the store. You can pay with them. You. Can order at the kiosk, but then what if I am at the kiosk and want a baked good? Or you can just go to the counter to pay. Too many options and gets confusing about where you should go.  It just seems disorganized. And I miss Joan’s on Third. It was the best stuff there!!

name: Cindy Bright
text: Option to add extras (like more broccoli on the cashew chicken)

name: Lorrie Dingman
text: We LOVE you guys and order for our office multiple times per week. The crusted chicken got rave reviews but the two salmon dishes were not their usual delicious selves.   I've ordered the salmon bowl at least 10 times and always love it.  Today the salmon was a bit over seasoned, one gal said hers was fishy, mine wasn't.  But the green beans for both of us weren't done.   I've always marveled at how you keep the beans al dente  without tasting raw and having such yummy flavor… Today they had a raw flavor and were not very good. This is the first time that I haven't thoroughly enjoyed this dish and we really love you guys, so I thought you would appreciate the feedback..  😊

name: Tuesday West
text: Service great, everyone was very friendly. Tried something new today from Gaby’s (the crispy chicken avocado wrap), not great. Ingredients were very fresh but overall dish was very heavy and somehow lacked flavor? Felt like an unhealthy dish trying to be healthy but just didn’t work out :(

name: David Russomanno
text: I didn't enjoy the pasta. It was oily ground beef.  Not something I get again could not even finish it.

name: Kelly Gonda
text: The crispy kale that I ordered was not crispy, and I also only received one of the two cookies that I ordered and paid for. Also, the french fries were undercooked. Sorry about all this but I’ve eaten at the restaurant a number of times and this is the first bad meal I’ve had.

name: Anne Vasey
text: The rice container was only 3/4 full so when we opened it, we looked at it like is this a fresh one??? the green beans were mushy and overcooked. The noodles were non-descriptive . The chicken was tough in the basil chicken dish. The Caesar saladwith added chicken was delicious. The beef and broccoli was good. I guess in a nutshell… I picked it up to take to my daughter and son-in-law’s house. They didn’t want the leftovers, they sent them home with me if that says anything. We have eaten there in the past and taken out in the past with no complaints but last night was not good.

name: Nicole D.
text: I got my daughter fried rice from social monk, and when I got home, noticed the bottom of the bag was soaking wet and then realized that it was a massive pool of grease that had leaked out of the rice.

name: MOJGAN TOOFAN
text: I’m not sure if it’s a new chef or a new approach to the breaded chicken but the chicken was overly breaded unlike before. It had a tremendous amount of breaded crust and the seasoning didn’t look well blended.

name: Joann Tong
text: I ordered online in advance so that I can get my food the moment I arrived.  Unfortunately, my order was processed after people who used the kiosk.  What’s the point of being proactive if the system puts you behind those who order in store.  I saw the couple come in after me and order after me.  The waiter at the store told me that’s how it works.

name: Lea Meeker
text: Hello, the meal was delicious... but upon arriving home and opening the containers to eat the orange chicken was not even half way filled. It was disappointing considering this was labeled a family meal. I don't normally respond to these reviews but felt like I should so future customers don't get jipped on their portions. (I have pictures to share if necessary.) Thank you 🙂

name: Yael Tobi
text: Pizza had no cheese or pesto

name: Azzie Fiouzi
text: More choices but overall good .  Bring back the Asian salad and minced chicken lettuce cups from social monk and have the bh cheese stir sandwhiches available

name: Adam Lock
text: From someone who has 20 plus years of service experience. The staff was plentiful and very responsive. Love that. The only minus’ I saw are I didn’t get Togo utensils. I had to come back in for them. Also, it was so busy I just felt like I was always in the way. There is not really a location for people to stand. That all being said, I’ll come back for sure. Keep crushing it.

name: Elizabeth Stanley
text: The pizza was cold and not very tasty. :(

name: Jessica Vasto
text: I ordered a new dish and it was burnt  and not ediable. Other dishes were great, as usual.

name: Dara Riordan
text: We had extra olives in our pizza when we said no olives

name: Amy Licht
text: Second time I’ve ordered a salad from Gaby’s and an ingredient was left out. Salami in first salad and dates in the second.  Salads are delicious….I’d live to enjoy them how Gaby intended them to be.

name: Alexandra Suchecki-Greene
text: The salad I ordered was pretty good, but the chicken was cold and awful.  The salami was tough to chew.  The pizza I ordered was good.  Overall a decent experience.

name: Victoria Nusser
text: I apologize for the recent review. In looking back, it was operator error

name: Victoria Nusser
text: Ordered a taco salad without chicken and cheese. I'm vegan, and definitely didn't want this on my salad, and yet my salad was full of it. This is my 2nd time there,  and I've had issues both times. You get 1 more try, which is generous. 3 strikes and you're out

name: Alycia Marrapodi
text: Love your food and your service by my salad was missing an ingredient (salami). Thanks

name: Amber Ryland
text: Two of the ordering kiosks were broken so the line was REALLY long and slow. I had limited time in between appointments so almost didn’t make it.

name: Patty Chiasson
text: food and staff was amazing as usual. Only issue was with the website. When I clicked on Menu at the top, it took me to a map and told me I wasn't in the area. And when I clicked on order for delivery, it said my office wasn't in the area (we're only a couple of miles away). We ended up picking up and all went well, and as I said earlier, the food was amazing!

name: Christine Yammine
text: The spring rolls were soggy bc the juice from the pickles you had in container the juice got on them 👎

name: Gary Gittelsohn
text: Charging for extra sauce when the amount you provide with order isn’t adequate really stinks.  Charging for rice in connection with entrees that ordinarily come with rice is also a new and very unwelcome idea.  Takeout cartons without venting holes ensures soggy batter and tasteless chicken.  I much preferred the standalone Social Monk formerly in the Neighborly space.  The trimmed down menu is a disappointment.

name: China Short
text: Still love it, but the quality of the Hotel Club sandwich has gone down significantly in the last few months. It’s good, but doesn’t blow me away like it did the first 5 times. Bread was a few days old and tough, less fillings etc…

name: Diane Gies
text: Gave you my phone number but did not get the promised discount. Paid for a side of rice that was not there. Tried to use absurdly complex coupon we got in the mail, which was not accepted. So, not particularly happy.

name: Dave Ghirardelli
text: Not much. The food was delicious and the quality is apparent. Actually delivery time was also good. A little quicker than usual.

name: Stanley Fisher
text: More selection

name: Victoria Nusser
text: The chicken cutlet should've been more tender. It was dry

name: Darlene Lee
text: I checked off no mango in my salad but there was mango in it

name: sara rudziewicz
text: Love love your food but your website sucks! Most times it won’t let you check out which is very disappointing.

name: Casey Reimer
text: I regularly pick up food from neighborly. I don’t have the website easy to sign in. It actually won’t let me, so I have to enter in my information every time, which is frustrating when I am in a hurry. I also would love some sort of reward for all my purchases, but I don’t know if you have a reward program or not & if there’s one I haven’t been able to get credit for all the times I’ve been there, at least 6 times in the last 2 months. All the food has been good except the orange chicken was the worst I have ever had. Love the pizzas, salads, sandwiches & kabobs. Workers are always friendly & helpful, even trying to help me sign in. It didn’t work.

name: Scott Schaffner
text: The pasta was swimming in sauce from the cheese shop. Just to much oil.   We have had Gabys several times and really enjoy it.

name: Vicki Fish
text: Make it easier to preorder

name: Lorrie Dingman
text: We ordered Rice with the Thai  basil chicken but didn't get any rice. It was also very spicy even though we asked for no dry chili flakes.

name: SHARON TAN
text: Ordered and picked up other co-workers orders. All scheduled for 11:15am pick up. Was not ready until 11:30. All the other orders were ready except for one order which had 3 orders. Not good.

name: Gina Tillman
text: It would be nice if someone was there to help.

name: Jenny Hyams
text: Bring back Joan's on Third pickles!

name: Ram Narayanan
text: Too much salt.

name: Sarah Brittan
text: Online platform was glitchy. In store experience and food was 5/5

name: Jooho Lee
text: If food is ready significantly earlier than the pick up time, it gets cold and soggy.

name: Heather McGonigal
text: Everything was good. I just wouldn't say it was amazing. The Orange Chicken was amazing but the kale was just ok & I love kale.

name: Coco
text: I had previously reached out that the last order I got was completely wrong and tasted old and bad… Justin kindly reached out to me and gave me four $15 credits toward future orders… Today was the first time I tried to use them in the first two codes came back with an error saying “this code has already been used” finally the third code worked for my order.  I will say that the food was much improved upon last time!! but the ordering experience was frustrating and the website was glitchy

name: Remy McCarthy
text: I couldn’t use auto fill on my iPad when making the online order but other than that, everything was great

name: Elizabeth Martin
text: Too expensive and too spicy

name: Beth Preece
text: So yummy!!  Just a few mushrooms in our bowls were so tough and big. Couldn’t eat them.  We love it there!  Eat there tooo much!

name: Aaron Levin
text: We got the wrong dressing with one of our salads

name: Lori Calabrese

name: Andrea
text: Open up aisle way where one orders.

name: Heather Holmes

Wasn’t whole thing so I picked apart and ate. Also very messy. Not sure I would order it again.   Saving salad for tomorrow.  Thanks for asking!

text: Don’t know

text: Love you guys and food and service is great! But sadly today 2 of the dishes had hair in it.

text: I asked for champagne vinaigrette but was given red wine.

name: Alexis Weikel
text: Staff is always friendly and pick up food is always ready and fresh. The to-go bags though need improvement. The handles break, as they did today as I was walking out of the restaurant. Whole bag ended up on the ground. Food inside was all ok but then as I was carrying the bag by its top, as the handles were broken, the bag rips. Invest in better bags!

I asked someone and they helped me get my drink from the refrigerator by the cash registers. I’m not sure if this was a mistake that it wasn’t already in the bag or if I am supposed to get it on my own in the future? Thanks!

name: Kevin Soufi
text: Only a few pieces of chicken was in mine

text: The salad I got was so good, I not only had it tonight for dinner but had also had it last night. I ordered the same thing tonight just switched up the dressing (both very good) but I think they forgot to add the extra chicken on tonight’s salad.

name: Mary Harrigan
text: Everything was delicious.  I would like the option to sub some kind of  green for the ancient grain/rice when ordering from Mini Kabob. Also, I would love to be able to add the Mini Kabob chicken thigh to the Greek Salad.

name: Ryan Dewey
text: On the plate options for mini kabob it would be nice to be able to substitute grains or rice for more of the cucumber, onion and peppers mix. Also chicken breast would be a great option not just chicken thighs with the skin on them.

name: Ryan Dewey
text: For the mini Kebob shop add the ability to just add a side of an extra kebob when ordering online.

name: Mike Wagman
text: Just wanted to share some quick feedback. I’m a big fan of your place and usually have an amazing experience. I came in again recently (March 30th), and the food was incredible. Everything was spot-on — seriously delicious.  That said, I did want to flag something from my last visit on March 17th. The hummus tasted a bit off — kind of bubbly and fermented — and unfortunately, it wrecked my stomach afterward. I checked in with a friend who ate there the day before (on the 16th), and she had the same issue with the hummus, so it might have just been a bad batch.  Just figured I’d let you know in case it helps. Definitely not trying to be harsh — I love what you do and will absolutely be back again soon!  Thanks!  Mike Wagman

name: Brian Goodkin
text: Food was OK - service, quality, and prep was great, but just not our thing. Got the Kid's Rice Bowl and prefer pita to lavash, breast meat to thigh meat.

Once we got home with our  take out pizza we realized the pizza was not like we have historically had.  It’s seemed like the dough did rise as usual, it was smaller and almost deflated.  Taste was okay but consistency left something to be desired.  I know you guys care about customer experience, so wanted to proved an honest review.

text: Pizza was horrible - the toppings were sparse and the dough was not even risen - it was dense and inedible.  We have eaten here at least 5 times in the past month or so and whoever was making the pizzas tonight was absolutely inexperienced and careless.

name: Coco
text: Two nights ago my friend got take-out of the social monk family meal kit, and I tried some of the food and it was SO delicious that I decided to get my own tonight. I was horribly disappointed with the quality of the food that I got tonight    All of the food was cold.  The chicken was soggy, the broccoli tasted terribly bitter like burnt garlic, the box of rice was only 2/3 full.   Considering I know how the food can taste it was incredibly disappointing to feel like I got food that had been sitting out for a long time… When I picked up my order, it wasn’t ready yet and a kind employee came over and got my name and told me that they were just finishing boxing it up and it would be right out to me… I got it within a minute and was home eating it in five minutes…. So I knew that was it wasn’t sitting around, packaged up for a while and must’ve been sitting around in the kitchen for a while. ….I couldn’t believe the difference in taste from my friends food a couple nights before… Definitely seems like consistency is an issue. I mean, absolutely no disrespect with this commentary as I know you are a new restaurant and I definitely will NOT be posting any kind of negative review on socials but I think it’s really important that you guys know how disappointed I was.

text: Food is great, but it was a tad better last time!

Nexf time, I’ll stick to the burrito bowl, love it!

text: It looks like the spicy chicken wrap is no longer on the menu. The regular crispy chicken wrap is ok, but not something I would order again. Both wraps were also a bit lighter on the ingredients than usual. The Greek salad was exceptional as always. These are minor criticisms, and I’m sure we’ll be back. Thanks for the lunch!

name: Sam Shap
text: Pizza was much smaller than before!

name: Krischen Sapalicio
text: Online website to order has issues. Took me several tries before I could send my order even though all the fields were completed.   No issues with pick up and the food itself.

name: Michael Lewis
text: I was a fan of Social Monk and was sad when I heard it was closing, and skeptical of the reorganization. I miss the Pad Thai on the menu but the Family Meal I just ordered was good.  Pros: Flavorful and good quality. Online ordering experience was good.  Cons: I had been emailed a coupon code “HINEIGHBOR” for $5 off $25. There was no expiration date in the email. But when I placed my order tonight, I was unable to use the coupon. This was a bummer. Also, when I picked up the food, the woman behind the counter told me to hold the bag by the bottom. When I got home, I noticed there was a huge rip in the bottom of the bag. Very lucky that the food didn’t fall out of the bottom.

name: Annette Conley
text: Online order said charge couldn’t go through, however my card was charged immediately and no order was made. Called and spoke with manager Daniel who said no order was placed and you don’t use STRIPE. I reordered and now I have 2 charges from STRIPE (and one order). Will deal with it later if first charge doesn’t fall off. Could be a glitch in your system.  The online ordering seems very straight forward, in spite of my little problem, I think it’s very clean and easy to use.   Please get rid of the kiosk ordering. It takes too long because people can’t figure it out… or can’t figure out they should decide before stepping up to the screen. Because they’re standing at a computer rather than a live person, they take their time and the lines get too long. Maybe customers should be required to pass an IQ test. If they pass, they can order from the kiosk, everyone else moves to the register for a human to handle it.    The food is terrific and exactly as ordered. Actually, everything we’ve ever ordered has been really good, packaged beautifully, and a credit to all the establishments you represent. I don’t know how you do it, but nicely done! Also, your staff is very friendly and professional.   Thank you for opening your first outlet in Westlake. I wish you success!

name: AMY WEBB
text: Website is easy. Store is very crowded with merchandise at center table and hard to navigate when crowded with people.  Staff is friendly! Food is good!

name: Shannon Shapiro
text: Nothing!

name: BRAD BUCKNER
text: The ordering process couldn’t have been easier, and was pretty much perfect. The particular dish I ordered didn’t knock my socks off, but I will be back for more to try something else. The idea of the place is fantastic. Wishing you success.

name: Kevin Davis
text: There was a small piece of blue plastic in our pasta. Other than that, everything was phenomenal. We’ll definitely be back!

name: Jessica Silvestri
text: No complaints! Order was ready when I arrived and was made correctly.

name: jacqueline black
text: There was a technical issue and when I placed online order it didn’t go through. Staff had to remake my order and was great about communicating what the issue was so I still around for 25 min wondering what happened.

name: Sue Moeller
text: More chicken to rice ratio

name: Janey Strouse
text: The sandwich was made wrong - could not eat. Asked for no aioli and they did no tomato’s instead. Couldn’t eat a$15 sandwich.

name: Meredith Luchs
text: My bowl when I was there last week was great. I did a pick up order at 2:30 today and brought it home. They had the wrong dressing I side. It was a burrito bowl and I had spice teriyaki sauce and some crunch stuff in the other little container. Very disappointed. It was also a bowl full of mostly rice and very little of the other stuff that I had enjoyed when we ate it there.

name: G J Antoniono
text: The new recipe for Kung Pao is not as flavorful as the prior. I also tried the Princess Chicken - good but a bit ginger heavy. Quality of chicken was very good. The Pasta Pomodoro was a little bland and the pasta was overcooked. The Jasmine Rice was good but I preferred the prior, stickier rice.  I like the new concept but I feel like it is very “brand-ey” and “concept” based and the execution (recipes) are not quite there yet.  (Also when I ordered the Chicken Kung Pao over the weekend, my special order was really screwed up. I ordered no chilis and no chili oil & the woman helping me at the kiosk entered the info but my order had lots & lots of chilis.)  Good luck  Greg

name: Michelle Yee
text: I received a loyalty award via email with a link to order online. When I clicked the link to order online, the award was nowhere to be seen and was not applied to my order.

name: Mario Del Pero
text: Dressing half full

name: Pratish Patel
text: Pizza seemed undercooked and very inconsistent from the time before. Service was great as usual, but disappointed in pizza

name: Lori Tabb
text: Our food was delayed, but I appreciated how it was handled. Thank you.

name: Sierra Srivats
text: Unfortunately both of our wraps had pickles on them even though we requested no pickles. We also ordered the dips and unfortunately did not receive the hot one. We were very disappointed that our food didn’t match what we ordered, especially for spending our money on the wraps and dip.

name: Patricia Gladstone
text: I ordered my wife's plate with all tzatziki, because she doesn't like hummus. And I just verified that in the email confirmation of my order. But the plate came with 1/2 and 1/2.

name: Shelley Baltimore
text: Easier to order

name: robyn halpern
text: Unfortunately, we didn’t get the salad that we ordered although we got a different salad we enjoyed it, but we also paid to get extra dressing and did not get that for our pizza. Still the food is delicious.

name: Justin Flam
text: Food was solid, tasted very good. I will say that the assembled product did not really resemble expectations based on the pictures. The sandwich I got was pretty flat. The toppings seemed like they were rushed (stack of pepperonis instead of even spread out, for example). The cookie was flat/concave compared to the thick, levain-style cookie displayed on the menu. Basically it was like looking at advertisements for McDonald's vs the real thing. Hopefully that example resonates.

name: TEST DO NOT MAKE
text: Testing.

name: Melanie Csellak
text: The extra jasmine rice ordered was missing from our order. Could we have it credited back to our card?

name: Alix Pickett
text: Allow us to order online through the QR code at the tables.

name: Jacob Hagen
text: My experience was great I ordered for pick up! The only feedback is the salad was missing the cilantro. But everything was delicious will be back!

name: Donna Carpenter
text: Open more than one kiosk to order when 40 people are in a line out the door in the cold with children

name: Jena Ferrara
text: Put all sandwich sauces on the side.  Sandwich was soggy when I got home.

name: Lisa Canale
text: No need to improve.  Liked it very much!

name: Nathan Pine
text: Order #32216506215301120.   One of the chicken bowls, the chicken was raw in the middle. I have pictures to show it. First time ordering and we were really excited. This was disappointing, especially since the chicken is the main part of the meal. Please make this right.

name: Tamara Morin
text: The one GG dressing really isn’t enough for a whole salad and i wan’t aware of this. I think offering 1 extra free of charge seems fair.

name: Darren Claypool
text: Food is great, people are very nice and welcoming. The online website ordering is a bit frustrating. I have had to place all the orders as my partners phone gets stuck at checkout. This time the checkout/cart got stuck on cookies (said they were not available) but the cart would not let us remove them to move forward. I logged on created the same order but did not add the cookies and checked out fine. All good, just wanted to let you know. Glad you are here. Cheers.

name: Molly Selnick
text: Pizza was burnt.  Crust hard as a rock. Tomatoes on top so burnt they were chewy and inedible.  I have had this before and it was delicious.  This time was awful and much smaller than last time.  Please get back to the way it was originally.

name: miriam Luna
text: Please bring back your old social monk menu  Serving sizes are also smaller.

name: Monica Murrietta
text: The welcome to the neighborhood code “hineighbor” didn’t work for my visit. ☹️ Other than that, service is great and food was delicious!

name: Bob Fishman
text: Ordered 2 orders of Choc chip cookies, only got 1 order.  Also, we ordered 2 sandwiches, but they were each mislabeled as the other.

name: Julien Fagel
text: Chinese chicken salad from Joan’s please!

name: Marissa Majeno
text: I requested no aioli sauce and unfortunately my sandwich came with it. I was on a time crunch as I ordered this for my lunch while at work and couldn’t make it back in. I had to toss the other half.

name: Darren Claypool
text: First time, only one register was open which I get to help guests 1:1 but that backed the line up outside and two other screens/registers were off.

name: Chandra Grasteit
text: We want to ❤️ you - part of our order was missing & the food was bland.. disappointing. Only good part was the cookies that my son bought when he picked up the order. We were told last week there would be delivery & there was no option. Keep going & make tastier food.

name: Derek Yoder
text: My brother-in-law and I both ordered the crispy chicken sandwich. My sandwich was dry and had different bread than his.  That said, his sandwich was really good (I had a bite) so unsure what happened with the bread … and if my fried chicken maybe was just thinner too

name: Corie Burton
text: I received the wrong order. My name was on the bag, the sticker on the sandwich said the correct name of the order (salad sandwich), but the sandwich I received had salami/prosciutto/ham in it.

name: Trena D'Amore
text: Everything was great

name: Michelle Fenton
text: I’m glad I checked my salad before because it was missing a key ingredient. It was happily fixed and salad was delicious!!

name: Ryan Giunta
text: Quite a bottleneck at the front entryway the last 2 times and our pick up order today had an incorrect item (wrong salad received which was loaded with garlic which my wife does not like), but we didn't know until we got home

name: Melanie B
text: -would be great to have more screens to order on. Long line  -I liked my mushroom teriyaki bowl from Gaby but could be improved 1) slaw was too saucy 2) mushrooms were a bit soggy. Would be better if they were browned with a bit of a crunch

name: Stephanie Kernochan
text: Better set up for pick up orders...it is confusing.

name: Tina Grumney
text: Place to sit while waiting for takeout/takehome.

name: Nicholas Riso
text: The food was fantastic. But I got a two pack of cookies for $10 that they never put in the bag. Bummed to get home and see that I paid for those items and they are missing.

name: Sierra Srivats
text: Regular soda options or lemonade/iced tea

name: Cameron Schmitt
text: Only one order kiosk open Pizza wasn’t sliced  No plates   Cheesy pasta was very good  Mushroom pizza was a bit too rich   Will try again thanks

name: Andrea Shapiro
text: Tzatziki and lemon aioli were bland. Lavash bread could be fresher. Pick up experience was great!

name: Michael Eveloff
text: Dear Neighborly,  I would try to more closely match the Social Monk wontons (in the soup).  Everything else was great.

name: Mikey Tonery
text: Pizza was a bit sloppy in presentation. Most of the cheese slid off, probably a touch too much sauce. But y’all were busy and it still tasted awesome

name: Stephen Sakulsky
text: Don’t use the ends of the heads of lettuces on the salads. I had 4.

name: Douglas Friedman
text: I tried to order a four pack of cookies and it would only let me choose chocolate chip. So we ended up ordering a 2 pack where we could choose different types of cookies

name: Jennifer Lee
text: Faster pickup time

name: Zachary Sider
text: Nothing everything was great

name: Lucy Ring
text: Pizza has way too much cheese MK plate is small and lacks flavor

name: Laura Glendinning
text: Forgot to omit chilies on chicken dinner sh

name: Joe Markiewicz
text: The cowboy taco salad had dill in it which isn’t labeled on the ingredients and I personally hate it. Wish I knew

name: Kenny Moreno
text: Food was delicious! Great variety and quality. It did take some time to order, as only one kiosk was open, but they shared they did that to ensure there would be enough seating. Staff was very nice and attentive. And love that there are goodies from Joan’s on third! Will definitely return!

name: Manda Gannon
text: Maybe more accurate pick up time,  by the time we picked it up the kids noodles were clumpy cold and a Bit oily.

name: Lindsay Nielsen
text: The sandwich we got was a little dry and needed more sauce. Everything else was great!

name: Katie Mazza
text: I ordered the Cesar wrap. It was not wrapped at all. Completely fell apart as i opened it. wrap was dry and fell apart

name: Masha Koft
text: Service

name: Amanda Benton
text: I paid $4.50 for bread and did not receive it. The food was delicious other than that.

name: Daniela Levine
text: Portions for the kabob plate was a little small for the price

name: Nick Lemieux
text: Portion for social monk sides is mall compared to other menu sides. Rice should not be extra for an asian meal that is always accompanied by rice. Joans on Third prefaced items are as expensive or more expensive then fresh made items.

name: Amin Razi
text: -floor leading to bathroom was very slippery -waiters were nice but checked in with patrons a little too frequently  -disappointed that mini kabob, who are known for their luleh kabob in glendale did not have luleh kabob on the menu. The ground beef kabob here also had pork in it which is not popular amongst the persian community. -similarly, bev hills cheese shop, who are known for their delicious sandwiches did not have any sandwiches on the menu lol.  -the kiosk system creates major bottlenecks in line. Staff should encourage online orders.  -some items in the dine in menu were not included in the online order menu -the dine in menu did not have prices -please offer more cookies :)

name: Bryan Breverman
text: Chicken in LA Chop would be easier to eat if it was smaller bites, but overall everything was good.

name: miriam Luna
text: Add more noodles to the bokchoy soup from Social Monk and bring back your old menu. We miss the Pad Thai!

name: Frank Leggio, III
text: Very cool concept. Staff is very welcoming. Food is good. The wraps from Mini Kabob would be better if the chicken was pulled, sliced or Schwarma. The thighs are not the best consistency and felt a bit fatty. It would be cool if the mini restaurants were alternated on a seasonal basis.

name: Angella Lopez
text: More ordering Kiosks open! But that’s it. Everything else was fabulous!

name: Terese McNamee
text: Need an exoeditor on togo. Someone took my order

name: Ali Duffy
text: Part of my pickup order was missing an item and there’s no option to speak with a person on the phone and I don’t have the time to go back in the holiday traffic just to get it fixed. I’m not thrilled I had to leave a message and expect I won’t get resolution.  Food was good, but my Caesar club was wrapped very loosely and fell apart immediately. Not sure I’ll go back for a while.

name: Barry Rodich
text: More options for online ordering

name: Jennifer Langsdale
text: There was a long line at the entrance and an open kiosk - customers weren’t being directed to try to use the kiosk on their own. I waited for a while and ended up going to my car to order online and waiting in my car.

name: Michelle Paymard
text: The food was good!  The online ordering was a bit difficult to checkout.  It would be nice if there was Apple Pay or pay pal.  Please bring back the Mongolian beef from Social Monk!

name: Sara Mouser
text: More kids options like a crispy chicken (chicken nuggets), veggies with dip, fruit cups, etc. I didn’t go inside, my husband did to grab the order, but it sounded like there might have been more options available that weren’t online. Sushi option would make this place aces. My fam is so picky so I love that I can experience variety while they stick to the basics. Congrats!

name: Gina Longo
text: It’s a little bit cramped inside

name: Ashley Pool
text: Great communication and delivery! Just a bit dissapointed in the choice of oils used. The brand seems to really push good ingredients and healthy options but the use of seed oils and vegetable oils is disappointing and reduces quality. Would love to see you push for all seed oil free etc. and go for olive, avocado, coconut oil and/or butter/tallow.

name: Melissa Weiner
text: Pesto Pasta was way too saucy and greasy
